summary: Cathy O'Neil shares her excitement about the first day of the Lede Program at Columbia Journalism School, a data journalism training initiative. The program kicked off with 17 students learning cloud computing basics by setting up Amazon EC2 servers. The day included a talk on technological determinism and the ethics of reproducibility, followed by hands-on Python exercises. The post conveys pure enthusiasm for teaching data skills to journalists.
key_points: The Lede Program launched with 17 students at Columbia Journalism School
Students immediately got hands-on experience with cloud computing (Amazon EC2)
The curriculum emphasizes ethical considerations like reproducibility alongside technical skills
Course materials are open and available on GitHub
money_quotes: After introducing ourselves – and the 17 students are each amazing – we each fired up an EC2 server on the Amazon cloud
In other words, we rocked out.
key_insight: Data journalism education should combine hands-on technical training with ethical frameworks from day one.
